stories.title: Fermi America to seek 5GW permit, expanding Texas AI campus to 17GW
stories.description: The filing follows TCEQ's approval of a 6GW gas permit two weeks ago for Project Matador, which the company says would be the world's largest private grid.

stories.title: US seeks to sell seized tanker after $47M in costs
stories.description: Federal prosecutors asked a judge Thursday for permission to sell a confiscated Venezuelan oil tanker and its cargo after maintenance costs far exceeded the ship's value.

stories.title: Vance privately 'skeptical' about Iran war before it began
stories.description: Administration officials told Politico the vice president was "skeptical," "worried about success," and "against" the conflict before falling in line.

stories.title: Zelensky calls Ukraine-Russia peace talks a 'soap opera' as venue dispute stalls process
stories.description: The U.S., Ukraine and Russia cannot agree on where or when to meet, with the Iran war keeping American officials from traveling abroad.

stories.title: Judge blocks Trump from forcing colleges to submit race data
stories.description: A federal judge in Boston extended the compliance deadline to March 25 after 17 Democratic state attorneys general sued to halt the requirement.

stories.title: Gulf states lose $15B in energy revenue as Hormuz blockade nears third week
stories.description: The Financial Times reports Saudi Arabia alone has lost $4.5 billion, with $10.7 billion in oil and LNG cargo stranded on ships unable to depart.

stories.title: Third Iranian missile enters Turkish airspace as Ankara pushes for ceasefire talks
stories.description: Turkey is actively mediating between Washington and Tehran even as it intercepts Iranian ballistic missiles crossing its border.

stories.title: Judge orders indefinite funding for CFPB, rebuking Trump administration
stories.description: A California federal judge found officials used flawed legal reasoning in a "transparent attempt" to shut down the consumer watchdog agency.

stories.title: ROTC students killed gunman who attacked Old Dominion University classroom, FBI says
stories.description: The FBI is investigating the shooting as terrorism after a convicted ISIS supporter killed an ROTC instructor and wounded two others Thursday.

stories.title: France, Italy open talks with Iran on Hormuz passage
stories.description: European nations are seeking to restart energy shipments as Gulf shipping remains paralyzed by the U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran.

stories.title: Federal judge blocks Trump plan to end TPS for Somali immigrants
stories.description: The temporary restraining order preserves legal protections for about 1,100 Somali nationals days before a March 17 termination deadline.

stories.title: Finland won't host nuclear arms in peacetime, Stubb says
stories.description: President Stubb sought to ease tensions after Finland's government last week moved to repeal a decades-old ban on nuclear weapons within its borders.

stories.title: Over 50,000 Americans return from Middle East since Iran war began
stories.description: The State Department said Friday that demand for charter flights is declining as the evacuation effort enters its third week.

stories.title: South Korea's PM meets Trump after $350B investment bill clears parliament
stories.description: The unannounced White House meeting followed the National Assembly's passage of legislation enabling Seoul to fulfill its U.S. investment pledge.

stories.title: Italy escorts damaged Russian LNG tanker drifting near its coast
stories.description: The crewless Arctic Metagaz, called a "ticking time bomb," is being shadowed by the Italian navy after an alleged Ukrainian drone attack.

stories.title: Sens. Warren and Van Hollen demand Hegseth's firing over Iran school strike
stories.description: The Democratic senators say the defense secretary's rollback of civilian harm protections contributed to the deadly Feb. 28 bombing of an elementary school.

stories.title: UAE says Iran must halt attacks before any mediation can begin
stories.description: Diplomat Lana Nusseibeh said the guns must go silent first but expressed confidence Trump will lead to a negotiated end to the war.

stories.title: Judge blocks DOJ subpoenas targeting Fed Chair Powell
stories.description: The ruling found "zero evidence" of a crime and called the probe a pretext to pressure Powell on interest rates or force his resignation.

stories.title: US offers $10M reward for tips on Iran's new supreme leader
stories.description: The State Department's Rewards for Justice program named Mojtaba Khamenei and nine other senior officials linked to the IRGC.

stories.title: Japan set to join Trump's Golden Dome missile defense program
stories.description: PM Takaichi plans to announce Japan's participation when she meets Trump in Washington on March 19, according to Japanese government sources.

stories.title: Michigan synagogue attacker lost family in Israeli airstrike, mayor says
stories.description: Ayman Mohamad Ghazali rammed a truck into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield, exchanged gunfire with security, and was killed at the scene.

stories.title: Midwest farmers rush to sell stored grain as Iran war fuels price surge
stories.description: Corn and soybean futures have each climbed roughly 6% since the conflict began, with soybeans topping $12 per bushel for the first time since mid-2024.

stories.title: Trump rejected Putin's offer to move Iran's uranium to Russia
stories.description: The proposal, made during a March 9 phone call, aimed to resolve the dispute over Iran's enriched uranium stockpile as part of a broader war settlement.

stories.title: Hegseth says 15,000 targets hit as war with Iran enters third week
stories.description: Explosions rocked Dubai on Friday as both sides intensified strikes, with oil above $100 a barrel and no diplomatic resolution in sight.

stories.title: Cuba confirms opening talks with U.S. after months of denial
stories.description: President Diaz-Canel said he directed negotiations alongside Raul Castro, marking Havana's first official acknowledgment of discussions with Washington.

stories.title: Trump signs orders to cut housing regulations, ease mortgage access
stories.description: The executive orders aim to speed up construction permitting and bring community banks back into mortgage lending ahead of the midterms.

stories.title: Bessent pulled from live interview to White House Situation Room amid Iran crisis
stories.description: The Treasury secretary told Sky News the U.S. Navy will escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz once military conditions allow.

stories.title: US military's AI-powered system struck 6,000 targets in Iran as war enters third week
stories.description: The Maven Smart System, built by Palantir and powered by Anthropic's Claude, compressed weeks of targeting work into hours amid growing civilian harm concerns.

stories.title: Democrats warn new Trump tariffs will cost households $2,512 this year
stories.description: A Joint Economic Committee study released Friday says the post-Supreme Court tariff regime represents a 44% increase over 2025 household costs.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates remains of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US submarine attack
stories.description: The bodies were flown from Mattala airport nine days after a US torpedo sank the frigate IRIS Dena off Sri Lanka's southern coast.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israeli strikes killed top Iranian nuclear scientist
stories.description: The claim came during his first press conference since the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran began Feb. 28, now in its 14th day.

stories.title: Trump says seizing Iran's Kharg Island 'not a top priority' but won't rule it out
stories.description: The president dismissed the question as "foolish" during a Fox News radio interview but called the oil hub "one of many considerations."

stories.title: Asian stocks slide as Iran war keeps oil near $100
stories.description: Iran's new supreme leader vowed to keep the Strait of Hormuz closed, rattling energy markets and dragging equities lower worldwide.

stories.title: Scientists decode 300-million-year-old DNA code hidden in plant genomes
stories.description: A massive study of 284 plant species identified 2.3 million conserved regulatory sequences, some predating flowering plants entirely.

stories.title: Japan's Katayama signals readiness to act on yen as currency nears 160
stories.description: Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said authorities will take "all necessary measures" as the yen hit its weakest level since July 2024 amid surging oil prices.

stories.title: ByteDance taps Malaysia partner to deploy 36,000 Nvidia Blackwell chips
stories.description: The TikTok parent is working with Aolani Cloud to build AI computing capacity outside China, skirting U.S. export controls on direct chip sales.

stories.title: Florida passes bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
stories.description: The legislation, which won't take effect until 2027, also eliminates student and retirement community IDs as acceptable forms of voter identification.

stories.title: Democrats unveil bills to cut taxes for workers, raise them on millionaires
stories.description: Federal legislation introduced by Sen. Van Hollen would eliminate income taxes for those earning under $46,000, funded by surtaxes on millionaires.

stories.title: GOP senators tell Americans to accept higher gas prices as cost of Iran war
stories.description: Gas has surged roughly 20% since the conflict began, with the national average hitting $3.58 a gallon as Strait of Hormuz disruptions roil oil markets.

stories.title: NASA clears Artemis II for April 1 moon launch
stories.description: Four astronauts will orbit the moon for the first time since Apollo 17, following a flight readiness review that resolved months of technical delays.

stories.title: Iran warns regional blackout would follow any US grid strike
stories.description: Tehran's top security official said darkness would let Iran "hunt down US servicemen," as threats expanded to oil and energy targets.

stories.title: Drones hit Dubai again as Iran's Gulf attacks enter 13th day
stories.description: Multiple drone incidents struck Dubai overnight and Thursday morning, including near the financial district, as corporate evacuations spread across the region.

stories.title: Aramco denies reports of talks to buy Ukrainian drones
stories.description: The Saudi oil giant called a Wall Street Journal report about negotiations with Ukrainian interceptor drone makers "inaccurate," saying no such procurement talks are underway.

stories.title: Polish president vetoes €44B EU defense loan bill
stories.description: President Nawrocki rejected legislation unlocking EU funds under the SAFE program, calling it a threat to sovereignty and sparking a clash with PM Tusk.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill Palestinians in Gaza market and camp during Ramadan
stories.description: At least three people were killed in attacks on a refugee camp and a marketplace, adding to hundreds of deaths since the October ceasefire began.

stories.title: Israel using tips from Iranian civilians to guide Tehran drone strikes
stories.description: A senior Israeli official says ordinary Iranians are submitting target locations via social media, helping guide strikes on Basij checkpoints in Tehran.

stories.title: Greene says GOP 'hijacked' by hawks as MAGA splits over Iran war
stories.description: The former congresswoman accused party leaders of enabling a neoconservative takeover, deepening fractures within Trump's coalition over the two-week-old conflict.

stories.title: Thune to bring SAVE America Act to Senate floor next week
stories.description: The voter ID bill faces long odds in the Senate as Democrats vow to block it and supporters fall short of the 60-vote filibuster threshold.

stories.title: Israel says it struck nuclear weapons site in Tehran
stories.description: The IDF said the Taleghan compound was tied to Iran's covert AMAD nuclear weapons program, as the war enters its third week.

stories.title: US envoy rebukes Czechs for falling short of NATO spending target
stories.description: Czech lawmakers approved a 2026 budget allocating just 1.7% of GDP to defense, well below NATO's 5% goal agreed at The Hague Summit.

stories.title: Trump weighs Jones Act waiver to curb surging fuel prices
stories.description: The 30-day exemption would let foreign-flagged tankers move oil and fertilizer between U.S. ports as the Iran war chokes global supply.

stories.title: Israel attacked Iran with no regime change plan, security sources say
stories.description: Nearly two weeks into the U.S.-Israeli bombing campaign, senior Israeli officials admit hopes of a popular uprising were based on "wishful thinking."

stories.title: US reopens DEA office in Dominican Republic after corruption probe
stories.description: The Santo Domingo office was shuttered in February after a senior agent was arrested on bribery and visa fraud charges.

stories.title: Canada says Iran war will dominate upcoming G7 foreign ministers' meeting
stories.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand said G7 allies will hold "a full-fledged conversation" on diplomatic efforts when they gather near Paris on March 26-27.

stories.title: Iranian drones hit Kuwait airport, Saudi oil fields as Gulf war widens
stories.description: Tehran's retaliatory campaign struck civilian and energy infrastructure across three Gulf states Thursday, with no casualties reported at Kuwait's airport.

stories.title: Israel drops charges against soldiers in Sde Teiman sexual abuse case
stories.description: Netanyahu praised the decision, saying Israel "should pursue its adversaries, not its heroic fighters." Rights groups condemned it as impunity.

stories.title: Israeli drone strike on Lebanese University kills two senior academics
stories.description: The faculty of sciences director and a professor were killed on the Hadath campus near Beirut's southern suburbs amid intensified Israeli bombardment across Lebanon.

stories.title: Israel strikes Basij militia checkpoints across Tehran
stories.description: The IDF says airstrikes targeted paramilitary roadblocks in Iran's capital as part of a broader effort to weaken the regime's grip on its population.

stories.title: Judge dismisses Gateway tunnel lawsuit after US releases frozen funds
stories.description: The $16 billion Hudson River rail project resumed after the Trump administration paid roughly $205 million it had withheld since last fall.

stories.title: Suspected Russian military drone found at mine in western Poland
stories.description: The device, likely from September's mass airspace incursion, is the first such discovery in western Poland, PAP reported.

stories.title: IDF kills Hezbollah's Radwan Force commander, IRGC missile chief in Lebanon
stories.description: Israel confirmed the killings as part of Operation Roaring Lion, which has seen nearly 200 strikes and over 100 Hezbollah fighters killed since March 2.

stories.title: Iran vows to 'drench Gulf in blood' if US attacks its islands
stories.description: Parliament speaker Ghalibaf issued one of Tehran's sharpest warnings yet as reports suggest Washington is weighing a seizure of Kharg Island.

stories.title: UN Security Council adopts resolution condemning Iran's attacks on Gulf states
stories.description: Resolution 2817 passed 13-0 with Russia and China abstaining, backed by a record 135 co-sponsors including India.

stories.title: Trump administration sues California over EV mandates
stories.description: The DOJ filed suit seeking to invalidate California's remaining zero-emission vehicle rules, calling them illegal under federal law.

stories.title: Iran launches drone strikes on Israeli bases as war enters day 13
stories.description: Iran claims its drones hit two airbases and Shin Bet headquarters, while coordinated Hezbollah barrages send millions of Israelis to shelters.

stories.title: States rush to set rounding rules as pennies vanish from registers
stories.description: Bills in six states await governors' signatures while Indiana signed one of the first rounding laws, as a federal fix remains stalled in Congress.

stories.title: Trump says US will 'make a lot of money' from rising oil prices
stories.description: The president framed surging energy costs as a financial benefit for the world's largest oil producer, even as gas prices hit $3.58 nationwide.

stories.title: Iran, Lebanon seek expanded UNESCO protections as strikes damage heritage sites
stories.description: At least four World Heritage sites have sustained verified damage since the U.S.-Israeli campaign began Feb. 28, UNESCO confirmed.

stories.title: China's mediation helps ease Pakistan-Afghanistan fighting, officials say
stories.description: A message from Xi Jinping to cease hostilities contributed to a reduction in cross-border strikes along the 2,600-kilometer frontier, Pakistani officials told Reuters.

stories.title: Anthropic asks appeals court to stay Pentagon blacklisting
stories.description: The AI company says the supply-chain risk designation could cost it billions in revenue, with over 100 enterprise clients raising concerns.

stories.title: Iran sets three conditions to end war with US and Israel
stories.description: President Pezeshkian demands recognition of Iran's rights, reparations, and guarantees against future aggression as the conflict enters its 12th day.

stories.title: Russia earned $6.9B in extra oil revenue during Iran war, analysis finds
stories.description: A new CREA analysis shows Moscow's fossil fuel earnings surged as the U.S.-Iran conflict disrupted global energy markets and drove crude prices above $90 a barrel.

stories.title: Pentagon tells Congress Iran war cost $11.3B in first six days
stories.description: The estimate, disclosed in a closed-door briefing Tuesday, does not include costs of the military buildup before strikes began Feb. 28.

stories.title: Washington Legislature passes millionaires tax, sends bill to governor
stories.description: The 9.9% tax on income over $1 million cleared the Senate on the session's final day and is expected to face legal challenges.

stories.title: US to release 172M barrels from oil reserve in historic IEA drawdown
stories.description: The decision reverses Trump's stance after days of downplaying the need to tap the SPR amid surging crude prices from the Iran war.

stories.title: US embassies across Middle East issue shelter-in-place orders as Iran war widens
stories.description: Multiple American embassies ordered citizens to shelter in place on March 11 as the conflict with Iran enters its third week.

stories.title: Israel plans military base in Somaliland to counter Houthis
stories.description: A strategic security partnership with the breakaway territory would give Israel an intelligence and operational foothold across the Gulf of Aden from Yemen.

stories.title: Trump rallies in Massie's district, calls him 'the worst'
stories.description: The president campaigned in northern Kentucky on Wednesday to boost primary challenger Ed Gallrein ahead of the May 19 GOP primary.

stories.title: US Embassy issues new alert as Iraq drone attacks hit 11th night
stories.description: Iran's IRGC launched what it called its heaviest strikes yet on Erbil and other targets as the embassy again urged Americans to leave immediately.

stories.title: Norway arrests three brothers over U.S. embassy bombing in Oslo
stories.description: The suspects, all Norwegian citizens of Iraqi background in their twenties, face terror bombing charges under the penal code.

stories.title: UN panel says Trump's rhetoric is fueling rights abuses
stories.description: The Committee on the Elimination of Racial Discrimination issued an urgent decision calling for a suspension of enforcement operations.

stories.title: CENTCOM tells civilians to evacuate Iranian ports along Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Iran responded by threatening to target all regional ports, as shipping through the strait has collapsed by 97% since the war began.

stories.title: Kilauea's 1,000-foot lava fountains force closures on Big Island
stories.description: Episode 43 of the ongoing eruption showered communities with volcanic debris, closing Highway 11 and parts of Hawaii Volcanoes National Park.

stories.title: Appeals court lets Trump third-country deportations continue
stories.description: The First Circuit temporarily blocked a lower court order that would have halted deportations of immigrants to nations other than their own.

stories.title: Romania approves US use of two air bases for Iran operations
stories.description: Parliament backed deploying refueling aircraft, surveillance gear, and 800 additional US troops at two bases for an initial 90 days.

stories.title: Anthropic launches policy institute amid Pentagon legal battle
stories.description: Co-founder Jack Clark will lead the Anthropic Institute, a new research body studying AI's impact on jobs, law, and the economy.

stories.title: Trump personally ordered DOJ to revive law firm sanctions fight
stories.description: The president told aides "I never signed off on that" after learning the Justice Department had moved to drop its appeals, according to the Journal.

stories.title: FBI warned Iran aspired to launch drone attacks on California from sea
stories.description: The bureau alerted California police in late February that Iran planned to use drones from a vessel off the West Coast if the U.S. struck Iran.

stories.title: Von der Leyen calls return to Russian energy 'a strategic blunder' amid Iran war
stories.description: The European Commission president told the European Parliament that 10 days of conflict have already cost EU taxpayers an extra €3 billion in fossil fuel imports.

stories.title: Trump renews threat to cut off trade with Spain over Iran base dispute
stories.description: Spanish PM Sanchez says he is "not worried" about U.S. reprisals after refusing to let American forces use bases for Iran strikes.

stories.title: New AI tools compress months of drug and protein research into days
stories.description: From drug synthesis to protein mapping, multiple studies published in recent weeks show machine learning dramatically cutting lab time and costs.

stories.title: Trial opens for 4 men charged in Haiti president's assassination
stories.description: Prosecutors told the jury that greed and a thirst for power drove the South Florida-based conspiracy to kill Jovenel Moïse in 2021.

stories.title: Trump says he doesn't 'know about' probe finding US hit Iranian school
stories.description: A preliminary military investigation found outdated intelligence data led to a Tomahawk missile strike on a girls' school that Iran says killed over 180 people.

stories.title: Experts accuse Bukele's government of crimes against humanity
stories.description: International experts filed a complaint with the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights, alleging systematic torture, killings, and disappearances under El Salvador's four-year emergency rule.

stories.title: Hyde-Smith wins Mississippi GOP Senate primary
stories.description: The Trump-endorsed incumbent defeated challenger Sarah Adlakha with roughly 83% of the vote, advancing to the November general election.

stories.title: Johnson opposes nation-building in Iran, breaks with Trump
stories.description: The House speaker said rebuilding Iran is "not our responsibility," a day after the president called the war "the beginning of building a new country."

stories.title: Iran launches drone strikes on Haifa refinery in second attack in three days
stories.description: The Tuesday strikes targeted oil, gas, and fuel storage facilities at Israel's largest refinery, deepening a tit-for-tat energy infrastructure war.

stories.title: Sen. Blumenthal warns US is on path to ground troops in Iran
stories.description: After a classified briefing, the senator said Russia appears to be "actively and intensively" aiding Iran as the Pentagon reported 140 U.S. troops wounded.

stories.title: AG Bondi relocates to military base amid cartel, Epstein threats
stories.description: The attorney general quietly left her D.C. apartment for a nearby military installation, joining a growing list of Trump officials seeking base housing.

stories.title: Fuller and Harris head to Georgia runoff for Greene seat
stories.description: Democrat Shawn Harris and Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller will face off April 7 for the deep-red 14th District seat.

stories.title: Sen. Kennedy apologizes for US strike on Iranian girls' school that killed 168
stories.description: The Louisiana Republican broke with Trump, calling the Feb. 28 bombing of the Minab school a "mistake" and expressing sorrow for the deaths.

stories.title: IRGC says Iran will decide when war ends, threatens Gulf oil blockade
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards dismissed Trump's claims the conflict is nearly over, warning they would halt all regional oil exports.

stories.title: Merz calls Israel's West Bank settlement push a 'big mistake'
stories.description: Germany's foreign minister made a surprise visit to Jerusalem to deliver the message directly, the first by a European diplomat since the Iran war began.

stories.title: Senate confirms Joshua Rudd to lead NSA and Cyber Command
stories.description: The 71-29 vote ends a nearly year-long leadership vacancy atop the nation's top signals intelligence agency and military cyber operations arm.

stories.title: White House urges GOP to drop 'mass deportation' talk
stories.description: Deputy Chief of Staff James Blair told House Republicans to shift messaging toward removing violent criminals as polls show eroding support.

stories.title: Musk says X Money will enter early public access in April
stories.description: The digital payment system, built into the X platform, offers P2P transfers, a Visa debit card, and a 6% yield on deposits.

stories.title: Ukraine says leaked Russian documents show 1.3M war losses
stories.description: Classified reports obtained by Ukrainian intelligence put Russia's own count of killed and seriously wounded at 1.315 million, Zelenskyy said.

stories.title: Witkoff likely to visit Israel next week to coordinate Iran war plans
stories.description: Trump's special envoy plans to visit Israel next week to coordinate the ongoing military campaign as war with Iran enters its second week.

stories.title: Iran war fuels global debate over shift to renewables
stories.description: EU ministers began emergency energy talks as oil price swings and disrupted shipping routes revive calls for clean energy as a security imperative.

stories.title: Shots fired at US Consulate in Toronto as attacks on American posts spread
stories.description: Police found roughly 10 shell casings and a damaged door but no injuries after a predawn shooting at the consulate on University Avenue.

stories.title: Trump announces first new US oil refinery in 50 years
stories.description: India's Reliance Industries is backing the Brownsville, Texas, facility, which will process domestic shale oil into gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel.

stories.title: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square files for $5B dual IPO on NYSE
stories.description: The combined offering would list both a new closed-end fund and Ackman's management company, with shares priced at $50 apiece.

stories.title: Netanyahu vows to keep striking Iran, says campaign is 'breaking their bones'
stories.description: Israel has tripled its strike tempo since the U.S.-Israeli offensive began Feb. 28, while Iran vows to fight on and rejects any ceasefire.

stories.title: NRC proposes cutting nuclear safety inspections by 38% under Trump orders
stories.description: Emergency preparedness inspections face the steepest cuts at 56%, with security and radiation protection hours also sharply reduced.

stories.title: Lawmakers in 11 states propose temporary bans on data centers
stories.description: Bipartisan legislation targets new data center construction over concerns about soaring electricity costs and water use driven by the AI boom.

stories.title: Apple now assembles 25% of iPhones in India
stories.description: India-made iPhones hit 55 million units in 2025, a 53% jump, as Apple accelerates its manufacturing shift away from China.

stories.title: Trump administration asks Israel to halt strikes on Iran's oil infrastructure
stories.description: The request marks the first time Washington has sought to restrain its ally since their joint military campaign against Iran began Feb. 28.

stories.title: ALMA detects high methanol levels in interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS
stories.description: The third known interstellar object to pass through our solar system has a chemical makeup unlike nearly any comet born here, researchers say.

stories.title: USDA monitors fertilizer market for price gouging amid Iran war supply crisis
stories.description: The department is watching for unfair markups as the Strait of Hormuz closure chokes off shipments weeks before spring planting.

stories.title: Macron, IAEA chief open nuclear energy summit in Paris
stories.description: World leaders gathered to discuss expanding civilian nuclear power amid rising energy demand, decarbonization goals, and geopolitical tensions.

stories.title: Oil's surge to $120 renews global push for clean energy
stories.description: The U.S.-Israeli war on Iran has knocked out a fifth of global oil and gas supply, spurring calls from analysts and governments to accelerate renewable energy investment.

stories.title: Cortical Labs unveils data centers powered by human brain cells
stories.description: The Australian biotech startup opened a biological computing facility in Melbourne and is building a second in Singapore with partner DayOne Data Centers.

stories.title: Group that beat Trump at Supreme Court sues over new tariffs
stories.description: The Liberty Justice Center filed suit on behalf of two small businesses, calling the president's 10% global tariff under Section 122 unlawful.

stories.title: US rejects Iran's claim that warship sunk by torpedo was unarmed
stories.description: INDOPACOM called Tehran's assertion "false" on Sunday as the two nations spar over the IRIS Dena, sunk March 4 off Sri Lanka, killing at least 84 sailors.

stories.title: Rep. Kevin Kiley leaves GOP, narrowing Johnson's House majority
stories.description: The California congressman becomes the only independent in the House, citing frustration with gerrymandering and hyper-partisanship.

stories.title: Kuwait's emir condemns Iran's 'brutal attack' in first address since strikes began
stories.description: Sheikh Meshal Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah broke his silence as the country buried two more border security officers killed in the conflict.

stories.title: Pentagon IDs 7th service member killed in Iran war
stories.description: Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, 26, died Sunday from injuries sustained in a March 1 Iranian strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Trump declares SAVE America Act as number one priority
stories.description: The president told House Republicans he won't approve any legislation until his election integrity package passes, threatening the CLARITY Act's timeline.

stories.title: IAEA chief says Iran's enriched uranium likely survived strikes at Isfahan
stories.description: Grossi told reporters over 200 kg of near-weapons-grade uranium was stored in Isfahan's tunnels before last June's attacks and probably remains there.

stories.title: Explosion damages historic synagogue in Belgian city of Liège
stories.description: Belgian officials condemned the early Monday blast as an antisemitic act targeting the Jewish community. No injuries were reported.

stories.title: Putin pitches Iran peace plan to Trump, backs new supreme leader
stories.description: On a day of diplomatic maneuvering, the Russian president proposed a swift end to the Iran war while pledging "unwavering support" to Mojtaba Khamenei.

stories.title: CENTCOM denies Patriot misfire, blames Iranian drone for Bahrain civilian injuries
stories.description: The U.S. called Russian and Iranian media reports that a Patriot missile struck a Bahrain neighborhood a "lie," saying an Iranian drone injured 32 civilians.

stories.title: IRGC offers Hormuz passage to nations that expel US, Israeli envoys
stories.description: Iran ties strait transit rights to a diplomatic ultimatum as France assembles an unprecedented naval force to reopen the waterway.

stories.title: RAF Typhoons down two drones over Jordan and Bahrain
stories.description: Defence Secretary Healey told the Commons the UK is now flying defensive sorties over the Gulf as HMS Dragon prepares to deploy.

stories.title: Trump says Iran was building granite-fortified nuclear site
stories.description: The president claimed Iran planned to attack the U.S. "within a week," contradicting what intelligence officials privately told Congress.

stories.title: Alexander brothers convicted of sex trafficking in Manhattan federal court
stories.description: A jury found the three wealthy siblings guilty after 11 women testified they were drugged and assaulted over more than a decade.

stories.title: Iran says it does not want to harm 'ordinary Americans'
stories.description: As the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran enters its second week, Foreign Minister Araghchi said Tehran's fight is with "Israel and its dupes in Washington," not ordinary Americans.

stories.title: Judge strikes down Trump's three-person NJ prosecutor setup
stories.description: A federal judge ruled the triumvirate running the New Jersey U.S. Attorney's Office was unconstitutionally appointed, threatening case dismissals.

stories.title: Sen. Booker proposes making first $75,000 tax-free
stories.description: The Keep Your Pay Act would more than double the standard deduction and expand child and earned income tax credits for working families.

stories.title: UAE calls for de-escalation after 1,400 Iranian attacks
stories.description: The Gulf state's UN envoy said four civilians were killed and 114 injured since the US-Israeli war with Iran began on Feb. 28.

stories.title: Trump and Putin discuss Iran and Ukraine in phone call
stories.description: The Kremlin said the Monday call, initiated by Trump, covered the Iran conflict and trilateral Ukraine settlement talks.

stories.title: US intercepts Iran signal that may activate sleeper cells abroad
stories.description: Encrypted transmission detected after Khamenei's death could be an "operational trigger" for covert operatives, a federal alert warns.

top.title: Fermi America to seek 5GW permit, expanding Texas AI campus to 17GW
top.description: The filing follows TCEQ's approval of a 6GW gas permit two weeks ago for Project Matador, which the company says would be the world's largest private grid.

top.title: US seeks to sell seized tanker after $47M in costs
top.description: Federal prosecutors asked a judge Thursday for permission to sell a confiscated Venezuelan oil tanker and its cargo after maintenance costs far exceeded the ship's value.

top.title: Vance privately 'skeptical' about Iran war before it began
top.description: Administration officials told Politico the vice president was "skeptical," "worried about success," and "against" the conflict before falling in line.

top.title: Zelensky calls Ukraine-Russia peace talks a 'soap opera' as venue dispute stalls process
top.description: The U.S., Ukraine and Russia cannot agree on where or when to meet, with the Iran war keeping American officials from traveling abroad.

top.title: Judge blocks Trump from forcing colleges to submit race data
top.description: A federal judge in Boston extended the compliance deadline to March 25 after 17 Democratic state attorneys general sued to halt the requirement.

top.title: Gulf states lose $15B in energy revenue as Hormuz blockade nears third week
top.description: The Financial Times reports Saudi Arabia alone has lost $4.5 billion, with $10.7 billion in oil and LNG cargo stranded on ships unable to depart.

top.title: Third Iranian missile enters Turkish airspace as Ankara pushes for ceasefire talks
top.description: Turkey is actively mediating between Washington and Tehran even as it intercepts Iranian ballistic missiles crossing its border.

top.title: Judge orders indefinite funding for CFPB, rebuking Trump administration
top.description: A California federal judge found officials used flawed legal reasoning in a "transparent attempt" to shut down the consumer watchdog agency.

top.title: ROTC students killed gunman who attacked Old Dominion University classroom, FBI says
top.description: The FBI is investigating the shooting as terrorism after a convicted ISIS supporter killed an ROTC instructor and wounded two others Thursday.

top.title: France, Italy open talks with Iran on Hormuz passage
top.description: European nations are seeking to restart energy shipments as Gulf shipping remains paralyzed by the U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran.

top.title: Federal judge blocks Trump plan to end TPS for Somali immigrants
top.description: The temporary restraining order preserves legal protections for about 1,100 Somali nationals days before a March 17 termination deadline.

top.title: Finland won't host nuclear arms in peacetime, Stubb says
top.description: President Stubb sought to ease tensions after Finland's government last week moved to repeal a decades-old ban on nuclear weapons within its borders.

top.title: Over 50,000 Americans return from Middle East since Iran war began
top.description: The State Department said Friday that demand for charter flights is declining as the evacuation effort enters its third week.

top.title: South Korea's PM meets Trump after $350B investment bill clears parliament
top.description: The unannounced White House meeting followed the National Assembly's passage of legislation enabling Seoul to fulfill its U.S. investment pledge.

top.title: Italy escorts damaged Russian LNG tanker drifting near its coast
top.description: The crewless Arctic Metagaz, called a "ticking time bomb," is being shadowed by the Italian navy after an alleged Ukrainian drone attack.

top.title: Sens. Warren and Van Hollen demand Hegseth's firing over Iran school strike
top.description: The Democratic senators say the defense secretary's rollback of civilian harm protections contributed to the deadly Feb. 28 bombing of an elementary school.

top.title: UAE says Iran must halt attacks before any mediation can begin
top.description: Diplomat Lana Nusseibeh said the guns must go silent first but expressed confidence Trump will lead to a negotiated end to the war.

top.title: Judge blocks DOJ subpoenas targeting Fed Chair Powell
top.description: The ruling found "zero evidence" of a crime and called the probe a pretext to pressure Powell on interest rates or force his resignation.

top.title: US offers $10M reward for tips on Iran's new supreme leader
top.description: The State Department's Rewards for Justice program named Mojtaba Khamenei and nine other senior officials linked to the IRGC.

top.title: Japan set to join Trump's Golden Dome missile defense program
top.description: PM Takaichi plans to announce Japan's participation when she meets Trump in Washington on March 19, according to Japanese government sources.

top.title: Michigan synagogue attacker lost family in Israeli airstrike, mayor says
top.description: Ayman Mohamad Ghazali rammed a truck into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield, exchanged gunfire with security, and was killed at the scene.

top.title: Midwest farmers rush to sell stored grain as Iran war fuels price surge
top.description: Corn and soybean futures have each climbed roughly 6% since the conflict began, with soybeans topping $12 per bushel for the first time since mid-2024.

top.title: Trump rejected Putin's offer to move Iran's uranium to Russia
top.description: The proposal, made during a March 9 phone call, aimed to resolve the dispute over Iran's enriched uranium stockpile as part of a broader war settlement.

top.title: Hegseth says 15,000 targets hit as war with Iran enters third week
top.description: Explosions rocked Dubai on Friday as both sides intensified strikes, with oil above $100 a barrel and no diplomatic resolution in sight.

top.title: Cuba confirms opening talks with U.S. after months of denial
top.description: President Diaz-Canel said he directed negotiations alongside Raul Castro, marking Havana's first official acknowledgment of discussions with Washington.

top.title: Trump signs orders to cut housing regulations, ease mortgage access
top.description: The executive orders aim to speed up construction permitting and bring community banks back into mortgage lending ahead of the midterms.

top.title: Bessent pulled from live interview to White House Situation Room amid Iran crisis
top.description: The Treasury secretary told Sky News the U.S. Navy will escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz once military conditions allow.

top.title: US military's AI-powered system struck 6,000 targets in Iran as war enters third week
top.description: The Maven Smart System, built by Palantir and powered by Anthropic's Claude, compressed weeks of targeting work into hours amid growing civilian harm concerns.

top.title: Democrats warn new Trump tariffs will cost households $2,512 this year
top.description: A Joint Economic Committee study released Friday says the post-Supreme Court tariff regime represents a 44% increase over 2025 household costs.

top.title: Sri Lanka repatriates remains of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US submarine attack
top.description: The bodies were flown from Mattala airport nine days after a US torpedo sank the frigate IRIS Dena off Sri Lanka's southern coast.

top.title: Netanyahu says Israeli strikes killed top Iranian nuclear scientist
top.description: The claim came during his first press conference since the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran began Feb. 28, now in its 14th day.

top.title: Trump says seizing Iran's Kharg Island 'not a top priority' but won't rule it out
top.description: The president dismissed the question as "foolish" during a Fox News radio interview but called the oil hub "one of many considerations."

top.title: Asian stocks slide as Iran war keeps oil near $100
top.description: Iran's new supreme leader vowed to keep the Strait of Hormuz closed, rattling energy markets and dragging equities lower worldwide.

top.title: Scientists decode 300-million-year-old DNA code hidden in plant genomes
top.description: A massive study of 284 plant species identified 2.3 million conserved regulatory sequences, some predating flowering plants entirely.

top.title: Japan's Katayama signals readiness to act on yen as currency nears 160
top.description: Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said authorities will take "all necessary measures" as the yen hit its weakest level since July 2024 amid surging oil prices.

top.title: ByteDance taps Malaysia partner to deploy 36,000 Nvidia Blackwell chips
top.description: The TikTok parent is working with Aolani Cloud to build AI computing capacity outside China, skirting U.S. export controls on direct chip sales.

top.title: Florida passes bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
top.description: The legislation, which won't take effect until 2027, also eliminates student and retirement community IDs as acceptable forms of voter identification.

top.title: Democrats unveil bills to cut taxes for workers, raise them on millionaires
top.description: Federal legislation introduced by Sen. Van Hollen would eliminate income taxes for those earning under $46,000, funded by surtaxes on millionaires.

top.title: GOP senators tell Americans to accept higher gas prices as cost of Iran war
top.description: Gas has surged roughly 20% since the conflict began, with the national average hitting $3.58 a gallon as Strait of Hormuz disruptions roil oil markets.

top.title: NASA clears Artemis II for April 1 moon launch
top.description: Four astronauts will orbit the moon for the first time since Apollo 17, following a flight readiness review that resolved months of technical delays.

top.title: Iran warns regional blackout would follow any US grid strike
top.description: Tehran's top security official said darkness would let Iran "hunt down US servicemen," as threats expanded to oil and energy targets.

top.title: Drones hit Dubai again as Iran's Gulf attacks enter 13th day
top.description: Multiple drone incidents struck Dubai overnight and Thursday morning, including near the financial district, as corporate evacuations spread across the region.

top.title: Aramco denies reports of talks to buy Ukrainian drones
top.description: The Saudi oil giant called a Wall Street Journal report about negotiations with Ukrainian interceptor drone makers "inaccurate," saying no such procurement talks are underway.

top.title: Polish president vetoes €44B EU defense loan bill
top.description: President Nawrocki rejected legislation unlocking EU funds under the SAFE program, calling it a threat to sovereignty and sparking a clash with PM Tusk.

top.title: Israeli strikes kill Palestinians in Gaza market and camp during Ramadan
top.description: At least three people were killed in attacks on a refugee camp and a marketplace, adding to hundreds of deaths since the October ceasefire began.

top.title: Israel using tips from Iranian civilians to guide Tehran drone strikes
top.description: A senior Israeli official says ordinary Iranians are submitting target locations via social media, helping guide strikes on Basij checkpoints in Tehran.

top.title: Greene says GOP 'hijacked' by hawks as MAGA splits over Iran war
top.description: The former congresswoman accused party leaders of enabling a neoconservative takeover, deepening fractures within Trump's coalition over the two-week-old conflict.

top.title: Thune to bring SAVE America Act to Senate floor next week
top.description: The voter ID bill faces long odds in the Senate as Democrats vow to block it and supporters fall short of the 60-vote filibuster threshold.

top.title: Israel says it struck nuclear weapons site in Tehran
top.description: The IDF said the Taleghan compound was tied to Iran's covert AMAD nuclear weapons program, as the war enters its third week.

top.title: US envoy rebukes Czechs for falling short of NATO spending target
top.description: Czech lawmakers approved a 2026 budget allocating just 1.7% of GDP to defense, well below NATO's 5% goal agreed at The Hague Summit.

top.title: Trump weighs Jones Act waiver to curb surging fuel prices
top.description: The 30-day exemption would let foreign-flagged tankers move oil and fertilizer between U.S. ports as the Iran war chokes global supply.

top.title: Israel attacked Iran with no regime change plan, security sources say
top.description: Nearly two weeks into the U.S.-Israeli bombing campaign, senior Israeli officials admit hopes of a popular uprising were based on "wishful thinking."

top.title: US reopens DEA office in Dominican Republic after corruption probe
top.description: The Santo Domingo office was shuttered in February after a senior agent was arrested on bribery and visa fraud charges.

top.title: Canada says Iran war will dominate upcoming G7 foreign ministers' meeting
top.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand said G7 allies will hold "a full-fledged conversation" on diplomatic efforts when they gather near Paris on March 26-27.

top.title: Iranian drones hit Kuwait airport, Saudi oil fields as Gulf war widens
top.description: Tehran's retaliatory campaign struck civilian and energy infrastructure across three Gulf states Thursday, with no casualties reported at Kuwait's airport.

top.title: Israel drops charges against soldiers in Sde Teiman sexual abuse case
top.description: Netanyahu praised the decision, saying Israel "should pursue its adversaries, not its heroic fighters." Rights groups condemned it as impunity.

top.title: Israeli drone strike on Lebanese University kills two senior academics
top.description: The faculty of sciences director and a professor were killed on the Hadath campus near Beirut's southern suburbs amid intensified Israeli bombardment across Lebanon.

top.title: Israel strikes Basij militia checkpoints across Tehran
top.description: The IDF says airstrikes targeted paramilitary roadblocks in Iran's capital as part of a broader effort to weaken the regime's grip on its population.

top.title: Judge dismisses Gateway tunnel lawsuit after US releases frozen funds
top.description: The $16 billion Hudson River rail project resumed after the Trump administration paid roughly $205 million it had withheld since last fall.

top.title: Suspected Russian military drone found at mine in western Poland
top.description: The device, likely from September's mass airspace incursion, is the first such discovery in western Poland, PAP reported.

top.title: IDF kills Hezbollah's Radwan Force commander, IRGC missile chief in Lebanon
top.description: Israel confirmed the killings as part of Operation Roaring Lion, which has seen nearly 200 strikes and over 100 Hezbollah fighters killed since March 2.

top.title: Iran vows to 'drench Gulf in blood' if US attacks its islands
top.description: Parliament speaker Ghalibaf issued one of Tehran's sharpest warnings yet as reports suggest Washington is weighing a seizure of Kharg Island.

top.title: UN Security Council adopts resolution condemning Iran's attacks on Gulf states
top.description: Resolution 2817 passed 13-0 with Russia and China abstaining, backed by a record 135 co-sponsors including India.

top.title: Trump administration sues California over EV mandates
top.description: The DOJ filed suit seeking to invalidate California's remaining zero-emission vehicle rules, calling them illegal under federal law.

top.title: Iran launches drone strikes on Israeli bases as war enters day 13
top.description: Iran claims its drones hit two airbases and Shin Bet headquarters, while coordinated Hezbollah barrages send millions of Israelis to shelters.

top.title: States rush to set rounding rules as pennies vanish from registers
top.description: Bills in six states await governors' signatures while Indiana signed one of the first rounding laws, as a federal fix remains stalled in Congress.

top.title: Trump says US will 'make a lot of money' from rising oil prices
top.description: The president framed surging energy costs as a financial benefit for the world's largest oil producer, even as gas prices hit $3.58 nationwide.

top.title: Iran, Lebanon seek expanded UNESCO protections as strikes damage heritage sites
top.description: At least four World Heritage sites have sustained verified damage since the U.S.-Israeli campaign began Feb. 28, UNESCO confirmed.

top.title: China's mediation helps ease Pakistan-Afghanistan fighting, officials say
top.description: A message from Xi Jinping to cease hostilities contributed to a reduction in cross-border strikes along the 2,600-kilometer frontier, Pakistani officials told Reuters.

top.title: Anthropic asks appeals court to stay Pentagon blacklisting
top.description: The AI company says the supply-chain risk designation could cost it billions in revenue, with over 100 enterprise clients raising concerns.

top.title: Iran sets three conditions to end war with US and Israel
top.description: President Pezeshkian demands recognition of Iran's rights, reparations, and guarantees against future aggression as the conflict enters its 12th day.

top.title: Russia earned $6.9B in extra oil revenue during Iran war, analysis finds
top.description: A new CREA analysis shows Moscow's fossil fuel earnings surged as the U.S.-Iran conflict disrupted global energy markets and drove crude prices above $90 a barrel.

top.title: Pentagon tells Congress Iran war cost $11.3B in first six days
top.description: The estimate, disclosed in a closed-door briefing Tuesday, does not include costs of the military buildup before strikes began Feb. 28.

top.title: Washington Legislature passes millionaires tax, sends bill to governor
top.description: The 9.9% tax on income over $1 million cleared the Senate on the session's final day and is expected to face legal challenges.

top.title: US to release 172M barrels from oil reserve in historic IEA drawdown
top.description: The decision reverses Trump's stance after days of downplaying the need to tap the SPR amid surging crude prices from the Iran war.

top.title: US embassies across Middle East issue shelter-in-place orders as Iran war widens
top.description: Multiple American embassies ordered citizens to shelter in place on March 11 as the conflict with Iran enters its third week.

top.title: Israel plans military base in Somaliland to counter Houthis
top.description: A strategic security partnership with the breakaway territory would give Israel an intelligence and operational foothold across the Gulf of Aden from Yemen.

top.title: Trump rallies in Massie's district, calls him 'the worst'
top.description: The president campaigned in northern Kentucky on Wednesday to boost primary challenger Ed Gallrein ahead of the May 19 GOP primary.

top.title: US Embassy issues new alert as Iraq drone attacks hit 11th night
top.description: Iran's IRGC launched what it called its heaviest strikes yet on Erbil and other targets as the embassy again urged Americans to leave immediately.

top.title: Norway arrests three brothers over U.S. embassy bombing in Oslo
top.description: The suspects, all Norwegian citizens of Iraqi background in their twenties, face terror bombing charges under the penal code.

top.title: UN panel says Trump's rhetoric is fueling rights abuses
top.description: The Committee on the Elimination of Racial Discrimination issued an urgent decision calling for a suspension of enforcement operations.

top.title: CENTCOM tells civilians to evacuate Iranian ports along Strait of Hormuz
top.description: Iran responded by threatening to target all regional ports, as shipping through the strait has collapsed by 97% since the war began.

top.title: Kilauea's 1,000-foot lava fountains force closures on Big Island
top.description: Episode 43 of the ongoing eruption showered communities with volcanic debris, closing Highway 11 and parts of Hawaii Volcanoes National Park.

top.title: Appeals court lets Trump third-country deportations continue
top.description: The First Circuit temporarily blocked a lower court order that would have halted deportations of immigrants to nations other than their own.

top.title: Romania approves US use of two air bases for Iran operations
top.description: Parliament backed deploying refueling aircraft, surveillance gear, and 800 additional US troops at two bases for an initial 90 days.

top.title: Anthropic launches policy institute amid Pentagon legal battle
top.description: Co-founder Jack Clark will lead the Anthropic Institute, a new research body studying AI's impact on jobs, law, and the economy.

top.title: Trump personally ordered DOJ to revive law firm sanctions fight
top.description: The president told aides "I never signed off on that" after learning the Justice Department had moved to drop its appeals, according to the Journal.

top.title: FBI warned Iran aspired to launch drone attacks on California from sea
top.description: The bureau alerted California police in late February that Iran planned to use drones from a vessel off the West Coast if the U.S. struck Iran.

top.title: Von der Leyen calls return to Russian energy 'a strategic blunder' amid Iran war
top.description: The European Commission president told the European Parliament that 10 days of conflict have already cost EU taxpayers an extra €3 billion in fossil fuel imports.

top.title: Trump renews threat to cut off trade with Spain over Iran base dispute
top.description: Spanish PM Sanchez says he is "not worried" about U.S. reprisals after refusing to let American forces use bases for Iran strikes.

top.title: New AI tools compress months of drug and protein research into days
top.description: From drug synthesis to protein mapping, multiple studies published in recent weeks show machine learning dramatically cutting lab time and costs.

top.title: Trial opens for 4 men charged in Haiti president's assassination
top.description: Prosecutors told the jury that greed and a thirst for power drove the South Florida-based conspiracy to kill Jovenel Moïse in 2021.

top.title: Trump says he doesn't 'know about' probe finding US hit Iranian school
top.description: A preliminary military investigation found outdated intelligence data led to a Tomahawk missile strike on a girls' school that Iran says killed over 180 people.

top.title: Experts accuse Bukele's government of crimes against humanity
top.description: International experts filed a complaint with the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights, alleging systematic torture, killings, and disappearances under El Salvador's four-year emergency rule.

top.title: Hyde-Smith wins Mississippi GOP Senate primary
top.description: The Trump-endorsed incumbent defeated challenger Sarah Adlakha with roughly 83% of the vote, advancing to the November general election.

top.title: Johnson opposes nation-building in Iran, breaks with Trump
top.description: The House speaker said rebuilding Iran is "not our responsibility," a day after the president called the war "the beginning of building a new country."

top.title: Iran launches drone strikes on Haifa refinery in second attack in three days
top.description: The Tuesday strikes targeted oil, gas, and fuel storage facilities at Israel's largest refinery, deepening a tit-for-tat energy infrastructure war.

top.title: Sen. Blumenthal warns US is on path to ground troops in Iran
top.description: After a classified briefing, the senator said Russia appears to be "actively and intensively" aiding Iran as the Pentagon reported 140 U.S. troops wounded.

top.title: AG Bondi relocates to military base amid cartel, Epstein threats
top.description: The attorney general quietly left her D.C. apartment for a nearby military installation, joining a growing list of Trump officials seeking base housing.

top.title: Fuller and Harris head to Georgia runoff for Greene seat
top.description: Democrat Shawn Harris and Trump-backed Republican Clay Fuller will face off April 7 for the deep-red 14th District seat.

top.title: Sen. Kennedy apologizes for US strike on Iranian girls' school that killed 168
top.description: The Louisiana Republican broke with Trump, calling the Feb. 28 bombing of the Minab school a "mistake" and expressing sorrow for the deaths.

top.title: IRGC says Iran will decide when war ends, threatens Gulf oil blockade
top.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards dismissed Trump's claims the conflict is nearly over, warning they would halt all regional oil exports.

top.title: Merz calls Israel's West Bank settlement push a 'big mistake'
top.description: Germany's foreign minister made a surprise visit to Jerusalem to deliver the message directly, the first by a European diplomat since the Iran war began.

top.title: Senate confirms Joshua Rudd to lead NSA and Cyber Command
top.description: The 71-29 vote ends a nearly year-long leadership vacancy atop the nation's top signals intelligence agency and military cyber operations arm.

top.title: White House urges GOP to drop 'mass deportation' talk
top.description: Deputy Chief of Staff James Blair told House Republicans to shift messaging toward removing violent criminals as polls show eroding support.

top.title: Musk says X Money will enter early public access in April
top.description: The digital payment system, built into the X platform, offers P2P transfers, a Visa debit card, and a 6% yield on deposits.

top.title: Ukraine says leaked Russian documents show 1.3M war losses
top.description: Classified reports obtained by Ukrainian intelligence put Russia's own count of killed and seriously wounded at 1.315 million, Zelenskyy said.

top.title: Witkoff likely to visit Israel next week to coordinate Iran war plans
top.description: Trump's special envoy plans to visit Israel next week to coordinate the ongoing military campaign as war with Iran enters its second week.

top.title: Iran war fuels global debate over shift to renewables
top.description: EU ministers began emergency energy talks as oil price swings and disrupted shipping routes revive calls for clean energy as a security imperative.

top.title: Shots fired at US Consulate in Toronto as attacks on American posts spread
top.description: Police found roughly 10 shell casings and a damaged door but no injuries after a predawn shooting at the consulate on University Avenue.

top.title: Trump announces first new US oil refinery in 50 years
top.description: India's Reliance Industries is backing the Brownsville, Texas, facility, which will process domestic shale oil into gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel.

top.title: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square files for $5B dual IPO on NYSE
top.description: The combined offering would list both a new closed-end fund and Ackman's management company, with shares priced at $50 apiece.

top.title: Netanyahu vows to keep striking Iran, says campaign is 'breaking their bones'
top.description: Israel has tripled its strike tempo since the U.S.-Israeli offensive began Feb. 28, while Iran vows to fight on and rejects any ceasefire.

top.title: NRC proposes cutting nuclear safety inspections by 38% under Trump orders
top.description: Emergency preparedness inspections face the steepest cuts at 56%, with security and radiation protection hours also sharply reduced.

top.title: Lawmakers in 11 states propose temporary bans on data centers
top.description: Bipartisan legislation targets new data center construction over concerns about soaring electricity costs and water use driven by the AI boom.

top.title: Apple now assembles 25% of iPhones in India
top.description: India-made iPhones hit 55 million units in 2025, a 53% jump, as Apple accelerates its manufacturing shift away from China.

top.title: Trump administration asks Israel to halt strikes on Iran's oil infrastructure
top.description: The request marks the first time Washington has sought to restrain its ally since their joint military campaign against Iran began Feb. 28.

top.title: ALMA detects high methanol levels in interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS
top.description: The third known interstellar object to pass through our solar system has a chemical makeup unlike nearly any comet born here, researchers say.

top.title: USDA monitors fertilizer market for price gouging amid Iran war supply crisis
top.description: The department is watching for unfair markups as the Strait of Hormuz closure chokes off shipments weeks before spring planting.

top.title: Macron, IAEA chief open nuclear energy summit in Paris
top.description: World leaders gathered to discuss expanding civilian nuclear power amid rising energy demand, decarbonization goals, and geopolitical tensions.

top.title: Oil's surge to $120 renews global push for clean energy
top.description: The U.S.-Israeli war on Iran has knocked out a fifth of global oil and gas supply, spurring calls from analysts and governments to accelerate renewable energy investment.

top.title: Cortical Labs unveils data centers powered by human brain cells
top.description: The Australian biotech startup opened a biological computing facility in Melbourne and is building a second in Singapore with partner DayOne Data Centers.

top.title: Group that beat Trump at Supreme Court sues over new tariffs
top.description: The Liberty Justice Center filed suit on behalf of two small businesses, calling the president's 10% global tariff under Section 122 unlawful.

top.title: US rejects Iran's claim that warship sunk by torpedo was unarmed
top.description: INDOPACOM called Tehran's assertion "false" on Sunday as the two nations spar over the IRIS Dena, sunk March 4 off Sri Lanka, killing at least 84 sailors.

top.title: Rep. Kevin Kiley leaves GOP, narrowing Johnson's House majority
top.description: The California congressman becomes the only independent in the House, citing frustration with gerrymandering and hyper-partisanship.

top.title: Kuwait's emir condemns Iran's 'brutal attack' in first address since strikes began
top.description: Sheikh Meshal Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah broke his silence as the country buried two more border security officers killed in the conflict.

top.title: Pentagon IDs 7th service member killed in Iran war
top.description: Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, 26, died Sunday from injuries sustained in a March 1 Iranian strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia.

top.title: Trump declares SAVE America Act as number one priority
top.description: The president told House Republicans he won't approve any legislation until his election integrity package passes, threatening the CLARITY Act's timeline.

top.title: IAEA chief says Iran's enriched uranium likely survived strikes at Isfahan
top.description: Grossi told reporters over 200 kg of near-weapons-grade uranium was stored in Isfahan's tunnels before last June's attacks and probably remains there.

top.title: Explosion damages historic synagogue in Belgian city of Liège
top.description: Belgian officials condemned the early Monday blast as an antisemitic act targeting the Jewish community. No injuries were reported.

top.title: Putin pitches Iran peace plan to Trump, backs new supreme leader
top.description: On a day of diplomatic maneuvering, the Russian president proposed a swift end to the Iran war while pledging "unwavering support" to Mojtaba Khamenei.

top.title: CENTCOM denies Patriot misfire, blames Iranian drone for Bahrain civilian injuries
top.description: The U.S. called Russian and Iranian media reports that a Patriot missile struck a Bahrain neighborhood a "lie," saying an Iranian drone injured 32 civilians.

top.title: IRGC offers Hormuz passage to nations that expel US, Israeli envoys
top.description: Iran ties strait transit rights to a diplomatic ultimatum as France assembles an unprecedented naval force to reopen the waterway.

top.title: RAF Typhoons down two drones over Jordan and Bahrain
top.description: Defence Secretary Healey told the Commons the UK is now flying defensive sorties over the Gulf as HMS Dragon prepares to deploy.

top.title: Trump says Iran was building granite-fortified nuclear site
top.description: The president claimed Iran planned to attack the U.S. "within a week," contradicting what intelligence officials privately told Congress.

top.title: Alexander brothers convicted of sex trafficking in Manhattan federal court
top.description: A jury found the three wealthy siblings guilty after 11 women testified they were drugged and assaulted over more than a decade.

top.title: Iran says it does not want to harm 'ordinary Americans'
top.description: As the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran enters its second week, Foreign Minister Araghchi said Tehran's fight is with "Israel and its dupes in Washington," not ordinary Americans.

top.title: Judge strikes down Trump's three-person NJ prosecutor setup
top.description: A federal judge ruled the triumvirate running the New Jersey U.S. Attorney's Office was unconstitutionally appointed, threatening case dismissals.

top.title: Sen. Booker proposes making first $75,000 tax-free
top.description: The Keep Your Pay Act would more than double the standard deduction and expand child and earned income tax credits for working families.

top.title: UAE calls for de-escalation after 1,400 Iranian attacks
top.description: The Gulf state's UN envoy said four civilians were killed and 114 injured since the US-Israeli war with Iran began on Feb. 28.

top.title: Trump and Putin discuss Iran and Ukraine in phone call
top.description: The Kremlin said the Monday call, initiated by Trump, covered the Iran conflict and trilateral Ukraine settlement talks.

top.title: US intercepts Iran signal that may activate sleeper cells abroad
top.description: Encrypted transmission detected after Khamenei's death could be an "operational trigger" for covert operatives, a federal alert warns.
